acceptance_criteria.criterion: library.yaml declares opencode-native agent default dirs using the existing default_dirs convention
Agent path resolution for --harness opencode targets .opencode/agents/ for project scope and ~/.opencode/agents/ for global scope
library use --harness opencode dry-run JSON reports opencode agent target_paths and does not report .claude/agents/
Non-dry-run agent install/remove avoids dangling Claude-path artifacts for opencode harness installs
Existing claude_code, codex, and cursor agent behavior remains unchanged

tests.command: UV_CACHE_DIR=/private/tmp/codex-uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_library_py_installers.py -q -k 'opencode_agent or agent_harness_routing or library_yaml_declares_opencode'
tests.result: failed before implementation with 5 expected failures covering missing opencode dirs and Claude-path routing

tests.command: UV_CACHE_DIR=/private/tmp/codex-uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_library_py_installers.py -q -k 'opencode_agent or agent_harness_routing or library_yaml_declares_opencode'

scope_changes: Added only opencode agent default directory metadata and opencode agent install/remove path routing.
Did not add opencode to harness=all behavior.
Did not change paths.py or cursor agent support.
downstream_impacts: Project-scoped opencode agent installs now write .opencode/agents/<name>.md.
Global opencode agent resolution now uses ~/.opencode/agents/.
Agent remove accepts --harness so opencode cleanup targets the opencode artifact instead of the Claude artifact.
